title: eriogonum heermannii var. clokeyi
intro: Eriogonum heermannii var. clokeyi, also known as Clokey's buckwheat, is a variety of the Eriogonum heermannii plant, which is a flowering shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Clokey's buckwheat can be made into teas, salves, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
